pages.text_excerpt: News - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP Home News How I can help Newsletter Tours of Parliament Visits and Diary News Barrow Crime and Antisocial Behaviour Forum On Saturday, I held a public meeting for Barrow upon Soar residents to discuss their concerns about local crime and antisocial behaviour (ASB). I brought together the police and Charnwood Borough Council to join me… Read more Working with Charnwood Borough Council I held a really informative meeting with Rob Mitchell (CEO) and Jewel Miah (Leader) at Charnwood Borough Council. Many of the issues raised by local residents with me are to do with Charnwood so it… Read more Loughborough College It was good to have a really productive meeting with Loughborough College this week, disusing how we get the skills our local young people need and what local business needs to succeed. Read more Visiting the Loughborough Area Foodbank This morning, I visited the Loughborough Area Foodbank and saw up-close the incredible work they do for the most vulnerable members of our community. The food bank is a testament to the staff’s dedication and… Read more Meeting Our Local Police Force It was truly encouraging to meet with Chief Inspector Michelle and her dedicated team to discuss strategies for reducing crime and anti-social behaviour in our community. pages.title: How I Can Help - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP
pages.text_excerpt: How I Can Help - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP Home News How I can help Newsletter Tours of Parliament Visits and Diary How I Can Help MPs can assist with matters that fall under the direct responsibility of central government, including issues related to: Benefits, pensions, National Insurance, and other matters dealt with by the Department for Work and Pensions Immigration and other problems dealt with by the Home Office Tax issues involving HM Revenue and Customs Problems with the NHS Problems with the Child Maintenance Service or Child Support Agency School and closures Housing I understand how challenging and stressful housing issues can be for many constituents. While I don’t have control over local authority housing, social housing, or the private rental market – and I’m not qualified to provide legal housing advice – I can assist in presenting your case to Charnwood Borough Council. We can’t demand specific actions or alter decisions, but I can help highlight administrative errors. It’s important to note that transparent processes are in place to ensure everyone understands the rules. The Housing Options Service can provide Charnwood constituents with free advice relating to preventing homelessness and finding accommodation when homeless. For independent housing advice, you can contact: The Bridge Citizens Advice Charnwood Immigration I can make enquiries with the Home Office about existing immigration applications and point constituents in the direction of useful information and providers of advice. However, I cannot legally provide you with advice on how to prepare or submit an application – for this you will need to contact a registered immigration adviser: Equality Action (Loughborough) Citizens Advice Charnwood Search for registered providers on GOV.UK Planning MPs do not have any formal role in the planning process. Those responsible for decisions in the planning process – such as planning officers, councillors, inspectors, and ministers – must adhere to strict procedural rules. These rules ensure that their decisions cannot be influenced by private discussions with anyone outside the formal process. The government advises that “ MPs should not seek to lobby planning ministers privately ” and that only representations that are made available to all interested parties can be taken into account. Sometimes MPs may help constituents articulate their concerns on a planning application, or write to the LPA about an issue. Anyone, including MPs, can also request for a planning application to be called-in by the Secretary of State. In the planning system, it doesn’t matter who presents an argument – whether it’s an MP or a constituent. At every stage where public input is allowed, all comments are evaluated solely on their merits, regardless of who submits them. See the House of Commons library for more information. Ask for my help If you have an issue that you believe I can assist with, please reach out to the constituency office via email at jeevun.sandher.mp@parliament.uk or phone 01509 351051 . We will inform you if we can take on your case, need more details, or recommend scheduling an advice session with me. If you want my help, please ask clearly for it. For example, if you email the council, a government department or a utility company and copy me in, I will not respond unless you ask for my help in that email or separately. I do not have a office open to visitors , however I do hold in-person surgeries across the constituency. If you want to speak to me in person, you can book a surgery appointment . Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP for Loughborough, Shepshed and the Villages Site content Home News How I can help Newsletter Tours of Parliament Visits and Diary Policies Privacy Policy Acceptable behaviour

pages.title: Visits and Diary - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP
pages.text_excerpt: Visits and Diary - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP Home News How I can help Newsletter Tours of Parliament Visits and Diary Visits and Diary An MP’s schedule follows a rhythm when Parliament is in session – Monday to Thursday in Westminster, and Friday and Saturday in the constituency, visiting schools, community groups, organisations, businesses, and holding advice sessions. If you’d like to invite me to an event, it’s best to aim for a Friday during the parliamentary year, as other weekdays are typically reserved for Westminster. During recess periods, my schedule is more flexible, but please keep in mind that I receive a large number of invitations each month, making it impossible to attend every event. Additionally, my diary often fills up several weeks or months in advance, so early booking is essential. Please email me with an outline of your event, and we’ll be in touch to discuss how we can assist. Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP for Loughborough, Shepshed and the Villages Site content Home News How I can help Newsletter Tours of Parliament Visits and Diary Policies Privacy Policy Acceptable behaviour

pages.title: Contact Me - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP
pages.meta_description: Contact details for Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP. You can contact me by email, phone or letter.
pages.text_excerpt: Contact Me - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP Home News How I can help Newsletter Tours of Parliament Visits and Diary Contact Me Please remember to include your name and address if you write to me and would like a reply,. Parliamentary protocol prevents an MP dealing with enquiries from another MP’s constituency, so I will need to confirm you are within the Loughborough constituency. If you are not sure, you can Find Your MP on the Parliament website.. Please note that I do not have a office open to visitors , however I do hold in-person surgeries across the constituency. If you want to speak to me in person you can book a surgery appointment . Email jeevun.sandher.mp@parliament.uk Phone 01509 351051 Letter PO Box 11212 LOUGHBOROUGH LE11 9PU House of Commons LONDON SW1A 1AA Privacy policy If you contact me, I may need to store and use certain personal information for the purpose of responding to your query or providing help with an issue. For more details please see my Privacy Policy . pages.title: Surgery Appointments - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP
pages.meta_description: I hold in-person advice surgeries at different locations across the constituency which you can attend in person or via Zoom. You can book an appointment online.
headings: h2: Need to cancel or running late?
pages.text_excerpt: Surgery Appointments - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP Home News How I can help Newsletter Tours of Parliament Visits and Diary Surgery Appointments I hold in-person advice surgeries at different locations across the constituency. Surgery appointments are pre-booked only – I won’t be able to see you without an appointment. To make an appointment please follow one of the links below. For all sessions you can choose to attend in person or online via Zoom. Behaviour Policy There will be a security presence for your safety and mine. By booking an appointment you will be acknowledging my Acceptable Behaviour Policy . Need to cancel or running late? If you cannot attend your appointment, please let me know as soon as you can by calling my office or sending me an email , or by following the link in the email if you received one. This means I can make the appointment available to other people who need it. Appointments are 15 minutes long and run back-to-back. If you’re late I won’t be able to see you for the full 15 minutes, and I might not be able to see you at all. If you miss your appointment without letting me know, someone from my office will contact you in the next few days to ask if you still need to speak to me and give you the opportunity to book another appointment. If you miss a second appointment without letting me know, I may not accept further appointment bookings from you; if this happens I will explain how else you can ask for my help if you still need it. Book an appointment May Quorn – Quorn Football Club – Saturday 2nd May 2026 – book now Loughborough – The Arc Community Hub – Friday 29th May 2026 – book now June Shepshed – Charnwood Road Community Centre – Saturday 13th June 2026 – book now Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP for Loughborough, Shepshed and the Villages Site content Home News How I can help Newsletter Tours of Parliament Visits and Diary Policies Privacy Policy Acceptable behaviour
